title: Metric Alias Many-to-Many Relationship with System Source Tagging
description:
Refactor the metric alias data model to support many-to-many relationships between metrics and aliases, with system source tagging.

### Requirements
- One metric can have multiple aliases
- One alias can be attached to multiple metrics (same alias name may exist in different systems)
- Each alias is tagged with a source system: Salesforce, HubSpot, Snowflake, Tableau, or 'input' (manually entered)
- Metrics from unknown sources or manually entered are tagged as 'input'

### Data Model Impact
- Requires new alias model with junction table for many-to-many relationships
- Migration of existing aliases to the new model
- All existing alias functionality (merge, add from sources, assign) must be updated
acceptance_criteria: Aliases support many-to-many relationships with metrics
Each alias is tagged with a source system
Manually entered aliases are tagged as 'input'
Existing aliases are migrated to the new model
All existing alias operations work with the new model
ux_changes: Alias management UI updated to show system source tags and support many-to-many relationships.
middleware_changes: Refactored alias CRUD operations for many-to-many relationships. Updated all alias-related features (merge, convert, assign) to use the new model.
data_model_changes: New alias model with junction table supporting many-to-many metric-alias relationships with source system tagging. Migration script for existing aliases.
implementation_notes: This was a significant refactoring touching all alias functionality including merge, add from Salesforce, and dictionary conversion. Developer worked on a separate branch before merging to dev. Existing aliases were migrated to the new model.
comment_summary: Developer raised questions about system tagging when adding aliases from different contexts. Reporter clarified that aliases from unknown sources should be tagged as 'input' and the same alias name can exist from different systems. Developer implemented the new model on a branch, migrated existing aliases, and deployed to dev for review.
